---
title: "Editing reproducible documents"
subtitle: "Topic 2 · Day 6 · 2 hours"
date: last-modified
---

## Outline

::: {.incremental}
- What *reproducible* means in practice.
- Quarto vs R Markdown — one shared engine, two dialects.
- YAML front-matter: metadata, output formats, execution options.
- Chunk options: `echo`, `eval`, `cache`, `fig-cap`, `tbl-cap`.
- Publication-ready tables with `gt` and `knitr::kable()`.
- Export: HTML, PDF (via LaTeX), Word.
:::

## Anatomy of a Quarto document

````markdown
---
title: "Tourism nights in Spain, 2024"
author: "Your Name"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: true
  pdf: default
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

# Introduction
Regular prose. Markdown **works** as expected.

# Data
```{{r}}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)

nights <- read_csv(here("datasets","raw","eurostat-nights_monthly.csv")) |>
  filter(c_resid == "TOTAL", unit == "NR",
         nace_r2 == "I551", geo == "ES")

nights |> summarise(mean_nights = mean(values, na.rm = TRUE))
```
````

## Chunk options you'll use daily

| Option | What it does |
|---|---|
| `echo: false`      | hide the code, keep the output |
| `eval: false`      | show the code, don't run it |
| `include: false`   | run silently, hide everything |
| `fig-cap: "..."`   | figure caption (cross-referable) |
| `tbl-cap: "..."`   | table caption |
| `cache: true`      | skip re-runs until the chunk changes |

## Publishable tables · `gt`

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(gt)

nights <- read_csv(here("datasets","raw","eurostat-nights_monthly.csv")) |>
  filter(c_resid == "TOTAL", unit == "NR", nace_r2 == "I551") |>
  mutate(year = lubridate::year(TIME_PERIOD)) |>
  filter(year == 2024)

nights |>
  group_by(geo) |>
  summarise(total_nights = sum(values, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  arrange(desc(total_nights)) |>
  head(10) |>
  gt() |>
  fmt_number(columns = total_nights, decimals = 0, use_seps = TRUE) |>
  tab_header(title    = "Top 10 EU countries by hotel nights",
             subtitle = "2024 · Eurostat tour_occ_nim") |>
  tab_source_note("Source: Eurostat, author's elaboration.")
```

## Rendering

```bash
quarto render report.qmd              # to default (html)
quarto render report.qmd --to pdf     # to PDF
quarto render report.qmd --to docx    # to Word
```

## Best practices

- One `.qmd` per analysis — not one mega-notebook.
- Keep raw data untouched; scripts transform it into intermediate
  datasets.
- Use `here::here()` so your report renders on any machine.
- Commit the `.qmd`, not the rendered `.html` — the CI builds it.

# Final submission {background-color="#0067a2"}

Write a 3-page reproducible report on one tourism question of your
choice, using the Eurostat (or ISTAC) data already in
`datasets/raw/`. Render to HTML and PDF. Push the `.qmd` (not the
outputs) to your fork.
